subject: DemoWidget
object: under 0.5W when plugged in but powered off
evidence_span: standby power is under 0.5W when the device is plugged in but powered off

subject: DemoWidget
evidence_span: typical active power draw up to 12W during normal use

subject: DC Voltage Prediction based on CNN with Physics Information Embedding for MVDC Distribution Systems
object: a physics-embedded convolutional neural network (PECNN) for terminal dc voltage estimation in MVDC distribution systems
evidence_span: This paper proposes a novel physics-embedded CNN (PECNN), specifically designed for terminal dc voltage estimation in MVDC distribution systems with different converter control schemes.

subject: PECNN
object: a multi-channel combination layer and a physics operation layer
evidence_span: The PECNN is enhanced with two additional layers, including a multi-channel combination layer and a physics operation layer.

subject: PECNN evaluation
object: three MVDC distribution networks with different dc voltage levels and network layouts
evidence_span: Three MVDC distribution networks with different dc voltage levels and network layouts are used to verify the superiority of proposed PECNN compared to other NNs in different topologies.

subject: PECNN
object: the physical relation between voltage, current, and conductance in an MVDC network by reconstructing input voltage and current matrices
evidence_span: the intrinsic physical relation between voltage, current, and conductance in an MVDC network is embedded by reconstructing the input voltage and current matrices

subject: A Physics-informed Neural Network Method for LC Parameter Estimation in Three-Phase Inverter
object: a parameter estimation method based on the combined use of artificial neural networks and circuit analytical models
evidence_span: This paper proposes a parameter estimation method based on the combination use of artificial neural network and circuit analytical models, e.g., physics-informed neural network (PINN), for a three-phase inverter application.

subject: PINN parameter estimation method
object: DC-link capacitance and AC-side inductance parameters for a three-phase inverter
evidence_span: The DC-link capacitance and the AC-side inductance parameters can be used as feedback for control optimization and component degradation monitoring.

subject: PINN parameter estimation method
evidence_span: It does not require any additional hardware circuitry and can be well-trained based on a small training dataset.

subject: three-phase inverter case study
object: theoretical analyses, simulations, and experimental verifications
evidence_span: A three-phase inverter case study is presented with theoretical analyses, simulations, and experimental verifications.

subject: experimental test
object: 5.2% for DC-link capacitance and 14.7% for AC-side inductance
evidence_span: Based on the experimental test it shows a maximum error of 5.2% and 14.7% for the DC-link capacitance and AC-side inductance, respectively.
